metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that the two 'scale calibration' eval cases target the correct eval spec: the Pass-1 finder (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml) does not score severity/blast_radius, so the 'inflated-scale finding is suppressed' AC cannot be proven there — consider whether the cases (or additional verifier-eval cases) belong in plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml, which already has a discriminates_impact_levels scorer and impact-axis discrimination pairs.
findings.finding: The two new 'scale calibration' eval cases are routed to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml, but the Pass-1 finder does not emit severity/blast_radius — its scorers (recall_on_seeded_defects, no_fire_on_good_cases) test fire/no-fire, not calibration of severity attributes. The 'inflated-scale finding is suppressed' AC cannot be proven by the finder eval; the verifier eval (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml) already has a discriminates_impact_levels scorer and I1-high/I1-low pairs that directly exercise this axis.
findings.location: AC line 3 / What section (Behavioral proof runs on plan-review-finder.eval.yaml)
findings.suggested_fix: Route the suppression-direction case (inflated-scale finding is suppressed) to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml as a low_validity or low_impact case alongside the existing I1-high/I1-low pairs. The fire-direction case (genuine high-scale finding still fires) can stay in the finder eval as a recall case, but should have a criterion tag and an expect: finding label consistent with the finder's existing format.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Move (or add) the two scale-calibration eval cases to `plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml` (which already has `discriminates_impact_levels` infrastructure) so they exercise Pass-2 severity scoring — where `blast_radius` is actually emitted — rather than the Pass-1 finder that emits no severity.
findings.finding: The two calibration eval cases (genuine high-scale fires; inflated-scale suppressed) are directed at plan-review-finder.eval.yaml, but the finder (Pass-1) does not emit severity attributes — blast_radius and impact are produced by Pass-2 (plan_review_verifier.md). The plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml already carries a discriminates_impact_levels scorer and I1-high/I1-low pairs. Adding scale-calibration cases to the finder spec exercises the wrong component and will not prove the FP-3(b) ratchet works.
findings.location: AC #3 / What section — 'Behavioral proof runs on the EVAL-SPEC harness (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml: a `{high-blast-radius: true, pair: scale-calibration}` case whose finding has a genuine system-wide blast_radius (expect: high_impact) and a `{kind: false, pair: scale-calibration, mode: inflated-scale}` case where the finding inflates a local defect to system-wide because the subject matter is large (expect: low_impact, with severity_claim_justified=no). Wire them to the existing `discriminates_impact_levels` scorer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC3: Redirect the two scale-calibration eval cases to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml (which already has a discriminates_impact_levels scorer and severity-attribute infrastructure), or define a new scorer for plan-review-finder.eval.yaml that explicitly explains how a Pass-1-only output can distinguish 'genuine high-scale fires' from 'inflated-scale suppressed'.
findings.finding: AC3 routes the two scale-calibration behavioral proof cases to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml, but the Pass-1 finder structurally cannot emit severity/blast_radius (its prompt explicitly says 'Do NOT emit severity, confidence, or priority — a separate pass computes those'). The finder's existing scorers test whether findings fire (recall/false-fire), not their scale magnitude. An 'inflated-scale finding is suppressed' outcome is untestable at the finder layer.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3 / What section 'Behavioral proof runs' / src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml
findings.suggested_fix: Add the two cases to plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml as a new pair under the discriminates_impact_levels scorer: one case where the blast_radius/prod_impact genuinely warrants 'system'/'high', and one where a small-defect finding was inflated to 'system' and the verifier should mark severity_claim_justified=no. This is precisely what the existing verifier eval infrastructure is designed to test.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define at least one canonical grep-able phrase per anchor type (small-scale default, prohibited-reasoning, two-directional) so AC1's proof command can be written deterministically.
findings.finding: AC1's proving command ('grep the phrases in both files') references phrases that are never defined in the plan; an executor cannot write the grep without inventing the required wording.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'Proof: grep the phrases in both files.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Target phrasing (normative)' sub-section listing the exact strings, e.g.: small-scale default: 'Unless the plan explicitly states wide blast radius, assume a small-scale change.'; prohibited-reasoning: 'Do not infer severity from subject grandeur alone.'; two-directional: 'Scale can lower OR raise severity; both directions apply.' Then update AC1 proof to grep for these literals.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC1: Define the exact text (or minimum required phrases) to be added to plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md for each of the three anchors ('small-scale default', 'prohibited-reasoning examples', 'two-directional bar') so the grep proof command is executable without guessing.
findings.finding: AC1's proof command greps for 'small-scale default', 'prohibited-reasoning examples', and 'two-directional' as literal phrases, but the plan never defines the actual text these labels must expand into in plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md. Both files are confirmed to not contain these strings today. An implementer cannot draft the prompt additions without inventing the wording.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Anchor text (normative)' subsection to the plan listing the literal sentences or key phrases that must appear. For example: small-scale default: 'Most findings are LOCAL in scope; do not default to system-wide'; prohibited-reasoning: 'Do NOT infer severity from the subject's importance alone'; two-directional: 'Calibration cuts BOTH ways: do not suppress genuine findings AND do not inflate small ones'. The grep in AC1 then targets these exact strings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge the multi-file duplication of calibration anchor phrases and either (a) designate one file as the authoritative source with the others referencing it, or (b) add a note in CLAUDE.md / a dev doc that all copies must be updated together, so future maintainers know the coupling.
findings.finding: Calibration anchor phrases ('small-scale default', 'prohibited-reasoning examples', 'never raises') are to be hardcoded as literal strings in four separate files (plan_review_A1.md, plan_review_T5a.md, plan_review_verifier.md, plan_review_verifier_agentic.md) plus verify.py, with no single source of truth or mitigation for future drift. The plan's own AC requires grepping across all three verifier locations to confirm consistency, which signals the coupling risk — if any one copy drifts, reviewer calibration silently diverges.
findings.location: What section: 'FP-3(b)' bullet; AC item 2 (grep across 3 files)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a comment in verify.py's SeverityAttrs doc (and/or a CLAUDE.md note) stating: 'This phrasing is mirrored in plan_review_verifier.md and plan_review_verifier_agentic.md — all three must be updated together.' Alternatively, extract the canonical phrase to a shared constants/config location imported or referenced by all three.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the `SeverityAttrs.blast_radius` edit target is the `Field(description=...)` string at `verify.py` line 116 (there is no class-level docstring; `SeverityAttrs` is closure-local inside `verification_model()`).
findings.finding: The plan names 'SeverityAttrs.blast_radius doc in review_kernel/verify.py' as an edit target, but SeverityAttrs is a closure-local class defined inside the verification_model() function — it has no standalone class docstring. The actual writable doc surface is the Field(description=...) string at line 116. An implementer following the plan literally may search for a class-level docstring that does not exist.
findings.location: What (verified edit locations) / FP-3(b) bullet; verify.py line 116
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius doc in review_kernel/verify.py' with 'the blast_radius Field description in review_kernel/verify.py (the Field(description=...) at line 116 inside verification_model())' so the implementer has an unambiguous edit target.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define observable pass/fail criteria for each eval case in AC3 (e.g., 'the high-scale case produces at least one finding with severity >= HIGH; the inflated-scale case produces zero findings or all findings have severity <= LOW') so the eval run produces a binary, reproducible verdict.
findings.finding: AC3's success criteria for the two eval cases ('a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires; an inflated-scale finding is suppressed') do not define observable thresholds — 'fires' and 'suppressed' are unquantified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC3: 'a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires; an inflated-scale finding is suppressed.'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify expected outcomes in terms of the eval-spec schema, e.g.: case 1 (genuine): expected_findings: [{min_severity: 'MAJOR'}]; case 2 (inflated): expected_findings: [] or all findings severity == 'MINOR'. Reference the exact eval-spec field names used in plan-review-finder.eval.yaml.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the plan that the two new `plan-review-finder.eval.yaml` dataset entries are a CREATION (new gated behavior), distinct from the revert-eligible doc/prompt edits, and confirm they are in-scope for this story.
findings.finding: The two new eval dataset entries to be added to plan-review-finder.eval.yaml ('a genuine high-scale/high-blast finding still fires; an inflated-scale finding is suppressed') are CREATION of new behavior (new test fixtures that gate the build). This is out-of-scope per the rubric's CREATION classification, but the plan presents them as a plain AC item without flagging this scope distinction.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a positive rationale in the plan for why the calibration anchors belong in A1/T5a specifically (e.g. 'these criteria are uniquely prone to grand-subject inflation') rather than in the central finder prompt — or reconsider whether plan_review_finder.md is the right single injection point.
findings.finding: The plan provides no positive rationale for why the small-scale default and prohibited-reasoning anchors should be injected into the individual criterion files A1.md and T5a.md rather than into the central finder prompt (plan_review_finder.md), which is the single prompt every criterion pass uses and already contains a DECISIVENESS calibration section.
findings.location: What section — 'G-9: small-scale default + prohibited-reasoning examples + two-directional bar in plan_review_A1.md and plan_review_T5a.md'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] AC2: Specify the exact sentence(s) containing 'never raises' (or the full 'blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises' rule) to be added to both verifier prompts and to the SeverityAttrs.blast_radius Field description in verify.py, so the grep proof command is unambiguous and the co-location requirement is concrete.
findings.finding: AC2 requires the phrase 'never raises' to appear in both verifier prompt files and the SeverityAttrs doc in verify.py, but the complete ratchet rule ('blast_radius only LOWERS, never raises') is defined nowhere in the codebase or the plan. The SeverityAttrs doc in verify.py contains only 'local|module|system' with no directional constraint text. The plan references a processing rule by label only, without specifying the full sentence that must be inserted.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2 / plan_review_verifier.md / plan_review_verifier_agentic.md / src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/verify.py SeverityAttrs.blast_radius
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan a normative sentence such as: 'In each target location add: "blast_radius only LOWERS a finding's severity — it never raises it; a finding about a local file is at most local, regardless of the feature's reach."' Then the grep for 'never raises' is trivially verifiable.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
